chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْإِشَارَةِ وَ النَّصِّ عَلَى عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 68 – The Indication and the wordings upon Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (320/ 3)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (320/ 3)
text: إِنَّ الْحُسَيْنَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) لَمَّا حَضَرَهُ الَّذِي حَضَرَهُ دَعَا ابْنَتَهُ الْكُبْرَى فَاطِمَةَ بِنْتَ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) فَدَفَعَ إِلَيْهَا كِتَاباً مَلْفُوفاً وَ وَصِيَّةً ظَاهِرَةً وَ كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) مَبْطُوناً مَعَهُمْ لَا يَرَوْنَ إِلَّا أَنَّهُ لِمَا بِهِ فَدَفَعَتْ فَاطِمَةُ الْكِتَابَ إِلَى عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) ثُمَّ صَارَ وَ اللَّهِ ذَلِكَ الْكِتَابُ إِلَيْنَا يَا زِيَادُ

قَالَ قُلْتُ مَا فِي ذَلِكَ الْكِتَابِ جَعَلَنِيَ اللَّهُ فِدَاكَ قَالَ فِيهِ وَ اللَّهِ مَا يَحْتَاجُ إِلَيْهِ وُلْدُ آدَمَ مُنْذُ خَلَقَ اللَّهُ آدَمَ إِلَى أَنْ تَفْنَى الدُّنْيَا وَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ فِيهِ الْحُدُودَ حَتَّى أَنَّ فِيهِ أَرْشَ الْخَدْشِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn and Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Mansour Bin Yunus, from Abu Al Jaroud,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup>, when it presented to him<sup>asws</sup> what which presented (death), called his<sup>asws</sup> eldest daughter Syeda Fatima<sup>asws</sup> Bint Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>, and he<sup>asws</sup> handed over the wrapped Book and the apparent bequest. And it was so that Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> was with a stomach illness. The ones with him<sup>asws</sup> were not seeing except that he<sup>asws</sup> was with it. So Syeda Fatima<sup>as</sup> handed over the Book to Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al Husayn<sup>asws</sup>, then, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, that Book came to be to us<sup>asws</sup>, O Zayd’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘I said, ‘What is in that Book? May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Therein, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, is whatever the Children of Adam<sup>as</sup> would be needy to, since Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Created Adam<sup>as</sup> up to the annihilation of the world. By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! In it are the legal punishments, to the extent that in it is the compensation of the scratch’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn and Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn Isma‘il from Mansur ibn Yunus from abu al-Jarud from abu Ja‘far (a.s.) who has said the following. “When al-Husayn ibn Ali (a.s.) was about to leave this world he called his eldest daughter, Fatima and gave her a sealed document and openly before the people his directive will. Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.) was ill with some internal illness and the people would see as dying. Fatima then gave the document to Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.). Thereafter, that document, by Allah, came to us, O Ziyad.” The narrator has said that he asked the the Imam (a.s.). “What is in that document, May Allah take my soul in service for your cause?” The Imam (a.s.) said, “In it there is all that the children of Adam needed from the day Allah created Adam to the end of the world. In it there is the law of penalties and even the rules of compensation for an scratch on a victim’s body.”
